summary: Completed testing of sortable column headers feature for leads table. Backend API sorting (11 tests) and Frontend UI sorting (8 tests) all PASSED. The feature required a frontend rebuild to deploy the changes.

details: Default sort state (Date DESC with ArrowDown icon) - PASS
Click Name header sorts ASC (ArrowUp icon appears) - PASS
Click Name header again toggles to DESC (ArrowDown icon) - PASS
Click Email header sorts ASC - PASS
Click Status header sorts ASC - PASS
Pagination resets to page 1 on sort change - PASS
All 8 sortable columns have icons - PASS
All 8 sortable columns are clickable (cursor-pointer) - PASS

feature_verification.feature_name: Sortable Column Headers for Leads Table
feature_verification.backend_endpoint: GET /api/crm/leads with sort and order query parameters

frontend_components.state_management: sortConfig state with field and direction (lines 216-228)
frontend_components.handleSort_function: Toggles between asc/desc and resets page to 1 (lines 222-228)
frontend_components.fetchData_integration: Includes sort and order params in API call (lines 298-304)
frontend_components.table_headers: Sortable headers with onClick handlers and sort icons (lines 1296-1407)

sort_indicators.ArrowUp: Shown when column is sorted ASC
sort_indicators.ArrowDown: Shown when column is sorted DESC
sort_indicators.ArrowUpDown: Shown when column is sortable but not currently sorted (opacity-50)

critical_code_review_comments: Backend sorting implementation is correct - uses MongoDB sort with direction (-1 for desc, 1 for asc)
Frontend correctly sends sort and order params to API
Sort toggle logic works correctly (asc -> desc -> asc)
Pagination reset on sort change is implemented correctly
Sort icons (ArrowUp, ArrowDown, ArrowUpDown) from lucide-react are properly imported and rendered
notes: Frontend required a rebuild (yarn build) to deploy the sorting feature changes. The initial test showed headers without sort icons because the build was stale.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sortable column headers feature is fully functional. Backend supports sorting by 9 fields (created_at, fullName, status, priority, email, phone, amountLost, team_id, assigned_to). Frontend shows sort indicators (ArrowUp/ArrowDown/ArrowUpDown) and resets pagination to page 1 on sort change. Test file at /app/tests/test_leads_sorting.py can be reused for regression testing.
